concerns.text: SC-002 requires measuring the pixel-level reconstruction quality (FID) of the *sparse method's output* against the dense baseline. T017 describes calculating FID 'comparing generated frames against the held-out RealEstate10K reference set', which measures absolute quality against ground truth, not the relative trade-off between the two methods required by the success criterion.

SC-001 requires measuring the topological fidelity (WorldScore) of the dense baseline *against the sparse method*. T017 computes WorldScore for the dense baseline but fails to explicitly implement the calculation of WorldScore for the *sparse* method (or a unified equivalent) necessary to perform the direct comparison mandated by SC-001.

The spec's 'Assumptions' section states the dense baseline is 'provided as a pre-computed baseline' (external input). T008b and T016 treat the dense baseline as a dynamic artifact to be fetched/validated or loaded from a file implying prior generation, creating a contradiction on whether the dense baseline is an external input or an internal pipeline output.

FR-001 requires stratifying to 'ensure statistical power (n≥50)'. T009 hardcodes 'exactly 50 sequences per stratum' with a 'fail fast' condition if fewer are available. This contradicts the spec's flexibility for 'n≥50' and risks pipeline failure if the source dataset contains slightly more or fewer than exactly 50 sequences per stratum, rather than selecting a representative subset.

SC-003 requires measuring inference time to evaluate a '≥40% reduction' hypothesis. T021 only describes generating a report summarizing 'Inference time reduction' without explicitly defining the task to calculate the percentage difference and compare it against the 40% threshold to produce a pass/fail verification of the success criterion.

The spec's Edge Cases require the system to 'detect low feature density' in 'Fast' sequences and mark frames invalid. While T010 mentions this, T013 (Solver) must also handle the 'Unsolvable' flag for low-texture sequences as per Edge Cases, but the task description focuses on RANSAC failure without explicitly linking to the 'mark as invalid' requirement for the statistical analysis exclusion.
